articles.title: Over 6 lakh vehicles reach Shimla in past 24 days; jump in tourist inflow amid summer heat
articles.description: Summer travel: Shimla witnessed massive tourist inflow with over 6 lakh vehicles entering the hill town in 24 days. Authorities introduced traffic diversions, volunteers and zone-based monitoring as visitors escaped rising temperatures across north India.

articles.title: Breaking News: West Bengal Launches Detention Plan Amid Crackdown on Alleged Illegal Immigrants
articles.description: Political developments in West Bengal have intensified following the launch of a new crackdown initiative targeting alleged illegal immigrants. The ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) government, led by Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari as per the claims in circulation, has begun implementing its electoral promise of identifying and acting against individuals residing illegally in the state. As part of this initiative, “holding centres” have been set up across multiple districts, which function similarly to detention facilities. These centres are intended to temporarily house suspected illegal immigrants while verification and legal procedures are completed. Authorities claim the move is aimed at strengthening border security and addressing long-standing concerns over infiltration. Reports indicate that several individuals, including suspected Bangladeshi nationals, have already been shifted to these holding centres in districts such as Malda and Murshidabad. In one such facility located in Malda’s English Bazar area, multiple individuals, including women and minors, are reportedly being held under surveillance. Security arrangements at these centres include CCTV monitoring along with deployment of police personnel and civil defence volunteers. Officials state that individuals identified as foreign nationals without valid documentation will be processed under government guidelines, which include temporary detention for up to 30 days while their identity and legal status are verified. Those found to be residing illegally may face deportation proceedings. The political discourse around the issue has become increasingly sharp, with strong statements made during public rallies and campaign events emphasizing strict action against infiltration. Authorities have also highlighted the importance of maintaining law and order while ensuring due legal process is followed.

articles.title: Harmful mirchi tadka: 802kg fake spices found in Old Delhi
articles.description: Authorities in Delhi have busted an illegal spice manufacturing unit in Khari Baoli, seizing over 800 kg of counterfeit spices. The operation uncovered grinding machinery and a large quantity of non-edible adulterants, including industrial dyes, mixed with common spices like red chilli and turmeric.

articles.title: Thailand becomes first country to enforce 21-day Ebola quarantine
articles.description: Thailand has implemented a mandatory 21-day quarantine for travelers from the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda due to the Ebola outbreak. This measure, effective May 27, 2026, applies to those who have traveled from or passed through these nations within the last 21 days. The move aims to bolster disease control, with strict penalties for non-compliance.
